resources.string: Server is unreachable. Check your network or IP address.
Request timed out. The server took too long to respond.
Network error. Please check your internet connection.
Data parsing error. The server response is not in the expected format.
The server returned an HTML page instead of JSON. Check your proxy/SSO configuration.
Invalid credentials. Please check your API key or login details.
Access denied. You don\'t have permission to view this.
Resource not found on the server.
A server error occurred. Please try again later.
UniFi sites, devices, clients and network health
Use a UniFi API key. For cloud access use https://api.ui.com; for local gateway access use your console URL and enable Network API integrations.
No UniFi devices returned for this site.
No UniFi clients returned for this filter.
Demo data is local only. No UniFi API calls are made.
Use your Wakapi API key. You can find it or generate it in the Wakapi settings page.
Use your Crafty Controller username and password. Homelab authenticates with the official Crafty v2 API and stores the session token.
No servers found in this Crafty Controller instance.
No logs available for this server.
Use the server console command without a leading slash.
Use the Healthchecks API key and instance URL.
Use Dockhand local credentials. If MFA is enabled, insert the optional 2FA code.
Use your Technitium DNS admin username and password. If 2FA is enabled, add the optional TOTP code.
Use username, password, realm and optional OTP.
Use a Proxmox API token in the format user@realm!tokenid=secret.
Find the API key in Project → Settings. Read-only keys can only view data; full-access keys enable edits.

This companion app is proudly Open Source and developed in my free time, and I am a student, so if you found it useful, I gladly accept donations (any crypto on EVM chain is fine) at the following address:
Apply neon borders and brand gradients to Home service cards.
A new version of Homelab is available. Update now to get the latest features and improvements.
View logs to troubleshoot connection and parsing issues.
You will need to log in again by entering URL and API Key.
Ad and malicious domains are being blocked.
Add a new domain to the %s list.
Set a custom duration in minutes.
No query log entries match current filters
Enter a URL to auto-fetch the icon
No containers available for this environment.
No stacks available for this environment.
Set a 6-digit PIN to protect your app
Enter your PIN again to confirm
Your PIN and biometric settings will be removed. Anyone can access the app.
Enter your current PIN to continue
Use fingerprint or face unlock instead of entering your PIN every time.
Open Tailscale VPN to access your homelab securely from anywhere
No instances yet. Add one to connect.
This removes only this configured instance.
These logs capture requests, responses, and parsing errors.
No logs yet. Actions in the app will appear here.
No logs match the current filters
Leave blank to keep current secret when only label or fallback changed
Create an access token in Portainer under My account > Access tokens.
Use the password or API token configured in Pi-hole under Settings > API / Web interface.
Use your AdGuard Home admin username and password.
If 2FA is enabled, use an app token instead of your password.
Use a Pangolin integration API key. If using an org-scoped key (no root access), also enter your Organization ID.
Use your linux-update-dashboard API token and instance URL.
Are you sure you want to delete this category and all its bookmarks?
Are you sure you want to delete this bookmark?
Reverse proxy, tunneling and zero-trust network overview
No target is currently exposed for editing.
This will permanently delete the check.
Schedule is required for cron checks
Timeout is required for simple checks
This key can only view checks, so edit actions are hidden.
Use the email and password of your Nginx Proxy Manager admin account.
Two-factor authentication on Nginx Proxy Manager is not supported from the app. If 2FA is enabled on your account, login from the app may fail.
I agree to the Let\'s Encrypt TOS
Are you sure you want to delete this?
Leave blank to keep the current password.
No query log entries match current filters
No hosts found for this filter.
Delete %1$s and all related PatchMon data? This action cannot be undone.
Docker integration data is not available.
In PatchMon: Integrations → Scoped Credentials → create token with GET and DELETE.

1) Open PatchMon → Integrations → Scoped Credentials.\n2) Create token with Host permissions: GET and DELETE only.\n3) Leave Allowed IP empty (or add your client IP), then copy Token Key and Token Secret here.
Host cannot be deleted due to related data constraints.
Access denied: this IP is not allowed in PatchMon token restrictions.
Access denied: missing required PatchMon scopes.
Host not found. It may have already been removed.
PatchMon is busy (rate limit). Please retry shortly.
PatchMon server error. Please try again later.
No data available from Plex server.
Use the URL of your Plex Server and your X-Plex-Token.
Too many requests. Please wait and retry.
Your digital home, organized and simple.
Do you want to set a lock code to protect the app?
Use the Jellystat API key and instance URL.
Tracked watch time and recent trends from your Jellyfin usage.
Your service configurations are encrypted using AES-256. The backup file is protected by a password of your choice.
Enter the password used when creating the backup.
⚠️ Selected services will replace current configurations for those services only.
Choose a password to protect your backup. You will need it to restore.
Password must be at least 6 characters.
Decryption failed. Wrong password or corrupted file.
Choose Home and ARR services for this backup.
Keep your last Home/ARR backup selection for next time.
Use your Radarr API key from Settings → General.
Use your Sonarr API key from Settings → General.
Use your Lidarr API key from Settings → General.
Use your qBittorrent Web UI username and password.
Use your Jellyseerr API key and instance URL.
Use your Prowlarr API key from Settings → General.
Use your Bazarr API key from Settings → General.
Add URL only, API key is optional if your endpoint is protected.
Add URL only, API key is optional if your endpoint is protected.
Connect your services once, then open each dashboard for deep controls and automation actions.
1. Connect each ARR service with URL and API key.
2. Open a service dashboard to see health, queue and library status.
3. Run quick actions like search/sync/rescan directly in app.
Open Tailscale to restore remote connectivity for your media stack.
Secure tunnel is active for your media stack.
Add missing ARR services in one tap.
Add at least one ARR service or qBittorrent to unlock the Media dashboard.
No torrents match the current filter
Choose the profile and folder to use for this request.
Are you sure you want to delete snapshot \'%1$s\'?
Are you sure you want to rollback to snapshot \'%1$s\'? This will restart the VM.
The WebView could not verify the server\'s certificate.
Are you sure you want to delete volume \'%1$s\'?
No Ceph cluster detected on this node
Tasks from all nodes will appear here
Docker container monitoring with auto-restart, alerts, logs and image updates
Use your DockMon base URL and API key. Homelab sends it as an Authorization Bearer token.
Docker fleet management and deployment monitoring
Use your Komodo base URL plus an API key and API secret. Homelab calls the Komodo /read RPC endpoints.
Inspect services and run safe stack actions.
No services reported for this stack
Malicious traffic detection with IOC feeds
Use your Maltrail base URL. If the server has USERS enabled, add username and password: Homelab signs in via /login, stores the session cookie, then reads /counts and /events?date=YYYY-MM-DD.
Status monitoring with uptime, latency and certificate health
Use your Uptime Kuma base URL. If metrics are protected, enter the API key as the password, or use username and password for Basic auth.
Use a Pterodactyl Client API key. Generate one from your account page under API Credentials.
No servers found for this account.
Use a Calagopus Client API key. Generate one from your account page under API Credentials.
No servers found for this account.
TrueNAS SCALE/Community Edition 25.04 or later is recommended. Use a user API key with JSON-RPC WebSocket access; HTTPS or WSS is required.
TrueNAS API access requires HTTPS or WSS.
